section_name: Issuance of Title to Government Agency for Travel Trailer
text:
(a) The department by rule shall establish a process to automatically issue a title to a government agency for a travel trailer used by the government agency to provide temporary housing in response to a natural disaster or other declared emergency.

(b) Notwithstanding Section 501.004 (Applicability) (b)(3), rules adopted under this section may provide for the issuance of a title for a travel trailer described by Subsection (a) that is owned or operated by the United States or transferred to a state agency from the United States.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 501. Certificate of Title Act
##### § 501.0341 — Issuance of Title to Government Agency for Travel Trailer

(a) The department by rule shall establish a process to automatically issue a title to a government agency for a travel trailer used by the government agency to provide temporary housing in response to a natural disaster or other declared emergency.

(b) Notwithstanding Section 501.004 (Applicability) (b)(3), rules adopted under this section may provide for the issuance of a title for a travel trailer described by Subsection (a) that is owned or operated by the United States or transferred to a state agency from the United States.

*Added by Acts 2019, 86th Leg., R.S., Ch. 811 (H.B.2315), Sec. 2, eff. September 1, 2019.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: The department by rule shall establish a process to automatically issue a title to a government agency for a travel trailer used by the government agency to provide temporary housing in response to a natural disaster or other declared emergency.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: Notwithstanding Section 501.004 (Applicability) (b)(3), rules adopted under this section may provide for the issuance of a title for a travel trailer described by Subsection (a) that is owned or operated by the United States or transferred to a state agency from the United States.

history.raw: Added by Acts 2019, 86th Leg., R.S., Ch. 811 (H.B.2315), Sec. 2, eff. September 1, 2019.
